issues: small sprite size reduces sub-0.5s spottability
busy dark-red tile background creates visual noise around asset
no drop shadow or ground integration — slightly floaty appearance
diagonal orientation aids silhouette but small pixel count limits clarity at a glance
reasoning: The axe_battle_v02 sprite sits on the dark red tile section, roughly center-frame. Warm orange handle and metallic head provide reasonable hue contrast against the cool-dark dungeon tiles, making it identifiable within ~1s of directed attention. However, the sprite is small relative to the scene and the red-speckled tile texture generates noise that competes with the asset edges. Silhouette reads as a diagonal axe shape once focused, but is unlikely to pop within 0.5s against this background without the red arrow aid. Art style is consistent pixel-art matching the game's Echoes aesthetic — no jarring paste-in artifacts. No color confusion with other in-scene entities (the creature sits in a clearly framed box to the left). Overall: visible and stylistically native, but spottability is marginal due to size and busy surroundings.
